codeReview.summary: The RegisterRally matching game implementation is complete and follows project conventions. The component correctly implements a register-to-description matching game with 14 8086 registers, 8 random registers per game, score tracking with time/accuracy bonuses, and localStorage persistence. The implementation uses consistent patterns with other games (InstructionHangman, FlagFrenzy) and integrates with the unified game scores system.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The RegisterRally component follows the same patterns as InstructionHangman and FlagFrenzy for game structure, state management, and localStorage persistence. No convention gaps observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Component uses useCallback for handlers, useState with lazy initialization, localStorage for stats persistence, and updateGameScore for dashboard integration - all consistent with other games.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly implemented the game using only inline data (ALL_REGISTERS array) rather than referencing external processed data files. This is appropriate since there is no data/processed/games.json or equivalent - games are self-contained.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ALL_REGISTERS array with 14 8086 registers is defined inline in the component. No reference to external game data files.

summary: RegisterRally feature implementation passes review. The matching game correctly fulfills P4-GAME-003 with proper game mechanics (matching registers to descriptions), score tracking, localStorage persistence, and dashboard integration. No code issues or convention violations detected.
